key_strategies: Niche-first expansion (med students > students > professionals > entrepreneurs)
SEO-driven foundation for first 100K
Weekly consistency for 5+ years
Revenue transparency built trust and attracted business audience
High production quality as differentiator

How I Type Really Fast (11M+ views)
Honest Advice for Starting a YouTube Channel (4M+ views)

SEO is critical before 100K subscribers
Revenue transparency builds serious followers
Evolution is necessary to prevent stagnation

milestones.event: Book became cultural phenomenon, multi-clip strategy

key_strategies: Volume over perfection (3-7 videos per week)
Anti-production brand (simple black background = 'too busy to care')
Book as flywheel (book drives YouTube, YouTube drives book)
Quotable frameworks that spread via word of mouth
Shorts as top-of-funnel to long-form
breakout_videos: How to Make $100K/Month as a Nobody (5M+ views)
Various Shorts hitting 20-50M views
$100M Offers breakdown videos
lessons_learned: Credibility precedes content — have results before teaching
Volume creates surface area for algorithm discovery
Simple production can be a brand advantage
Named frameworks are more viral than raw advice

milestones.event: 4 years of near-zero growth, algorithm study
Money giveaway format created new genre

key_strategies: 4 years of obsessive algorithm study before results
Escalation strategy — each video bigger than the last
Thumbnail/title-first production (packaging before filming)
Reinvested all revenue into content quality
International dubbing in 12+ languages
breakout_videos: I Counted to 100,000 (25M+ views, first viral hit)
$456,000 Squid Game In Real Life (600M+ views)
$1 vs $1,000,000 Hotel Room (188M+ views)
current_status: 280M+ subscribers, largest individual creator on YouTube
lessons_learned: 4 years of failure preceded success — persistence is non-negotiable
Packaging (thumbnail/title) comes before content production
Reinvest everything — compounding quality creates exponential advantage
International dubbing is highest-ROI growth strategy for large channels

milestones.event: 100 Seconds became a meme, Code Report added

key_strategies: Format innovation ('100 Seconds of X' was genuinely new)
Extreme respect for viewer time — zero filler
Dry humor as differentiator in a serious niche
Niche-to-broad pipeline (Angular > web dev > all tech)
Consistent weekly schedule
breakout_videos: God-Tier Developer Roadmap (5M+ views)
The '100 Seconds' series as a whole
AI coverage videos (3-8M views each)
lessons_learned: Format innovation matters more than content quality improvement
Density > Length — 100 seconds of value beats 20 minutes of padding
Humor is a massive differentiator in serious niches
Be positioned when the wave comes (AI boom)

key_strategies: Stanford neuroscientist credential as unassailable moat
Extreme depth (2-3 hour episodes)
Protocol framework (science > actionable steps)
Consistent weekly cadence
Clip strategy for broader distribution
breakout_videos: Master Your Sleep (10M+ views)
Controlling Your Dopamine (15M+ views)
Guest episodes driving cross-audience discovery
lessons_learned: Credentials are the ultimate growth hack
Long-form can win in the short-form era
Actionable protocols > informational explanations
Long episodes are the product; clips are the marketing

key_strategies: Underserved niche selection (creator economy analysis)
Access as moat (top creator interviews built relationships)
B2B audience strategy (brands and agencies, not just viewers)
Documentary-level production quality
Network effects from interviews
breakout_videos: How MrBeast Built an Empire
Industry analysis during YouTube platform updates

Access to top creators compounds over time
B2B audiences generate outsized revenue per subscriber
Pivot when data shows a better opportunity

milestones.event: Pivoted to challenge format, rapid growth

key_strategies: Aggressive format pivot from lifestyle to challenge content
Series format (30-day penny series kept viewers returning daily)
Authentic emotional struggle during challenges
MrBeast collaboration pipeline
Relatable 'regular guy in extraordinary situations' persona
breakout_videos: I Survived on $0.01 for 30 Days (58M+ views)
I Tried Every Fast Food Breakfast (12M+ views)
lessons_learned: Pivot aggressively when you find what works
Series beat singles for subscriber growth
Authentic emotional reactions can't be faked
Being in the right network accelerates discovery

milestones.event: Crypto boom created explosion of targets

key_strategies: Rigorous documentation (receipts, screenshots, blockchain analysis)
Target selection — investigating people with large audiences
Documentary narrative structure for investigations
Controversy as distribution (targets respond publicly)
Consistent uploads between major investigations
breakout_videos: Investigating Logan Paul's CryptoZoo (40M+ combined views)
Various crypto/NFT scam exposures during 2021-2022
lessons_learned: Timing — the crypto boom/bust created perfect conditions
Investigate people with audiences (their audience discovers you)
Evidence > Opinion for long-term credibility
Establish authority before the market needs one

milestones.event: Went viral through raw, unedited training vlogs (~6 months)
Maintained daily uploads, cultural figure in fitness

key_strategies: Anti-production as brand (phone quality, no editing)
Daily upload volume creating viewing habit
Pure authenticity (no scripts, no optimization)
Controversy as fuel (extreme diet sparked debate)
Youth and demographic alignment with core YouTube audience
breakout_videos: No single breakout — the entire channel concept went viral
Multiple 'Day of Eating' and training videos hitting 3-8M views
lessons_learned: When the market zigs, zag — go anti-production when everyone is polished
Daily uploads create habitual viewership
Lack of strategy can BE the strategy when authenticity is scarce
Controversy drives free distribution through community debate

milestones.event: Expanded to philosophy and society topics

key_strategies: Art style as unassailable moat (40+ person art team)
Quality over frequency (1 video/month, TV-quality production)
Evergreen topics that generate perpetual traffic
Cross-cultural appeal through animation (no faces, no language barrier)
Emotional resonance beyond pure information
breakout_videos: The Immune System Explained (early viral hit)
Optimistic Nihilism (30M+ views, defined philosophical brand)
'What If' and 'Size of the Universe' videos (20M+ each)
lessons_learned: Build a moat nobody can cross — art style requires 40-person team
Infrequent + high quality can outperform frequent + average
Animation is borderless — no cultural limitations
Emotional science > informational science
